description: Initialize a Go CLI project for goralph with Cobra and Viper
steps: Create a Go module for the repository
Add Cobra root command for goralph
Add Viper-based config loading hooks
Run go test ./... successfully

description: Use the approved internal package layout
steps: Create cmd/goralph/main.go
Create internal packages for cli, config, db, git, prd, runner, runner/pi, and loop
Keep command wiring in internal/cli
Verify go test ./... passes with the package layout

description: Resolve the global SQLite database path from XDG locations with explicit overrides
steps: Use --db when provided
Use GO_RALPH_DB when --db is absent
Use $XDG_DATA_HOME/goralph/ralph.db when available
Fall back to ~/.local/share/goralph/ralph.db
Create parent directories when needed

description: Implement SQLite access using sqlc-generated queries and modernc.org/sqlite
steps: Add sqlc configuration for SQLite
Use modernc.org/sqlite as the database driver
Generate type-safe query code with sqlc
Avoid ORM-based persistence
Verify generated code builds

description: Implement Goose migration support with embedded migrations
steps: Create internal/db/migrations with Goose migration files
Embed migrations in the Go binary
Run migrations automatically before normal commands
Record migration versions using Goose metadata
Verify a fresh database migrates successfully

description: Provide database management commands
steps: Implement goralph db path
Implement goralph db migrate
Implement goralph db reset --force
Ensure destructive reset requires --force
Verify commands work with --db pointing at a temp file

description: Create normalized SQLite schema for projects, tasks, steps, progress, runs, and feedback commands
steps: Create project table with id, name, unique root_path, description, and timestamps
Create task table with project_id, category, description, status enum text, progress_report, and timestamps
Create task_step table with task_id, position, and description
Create progress table with project_id, optional task_id, optional run_id, summary, and timestamps
Create run table with project_id, optional task_id, runner metadata, session metadata, status, exit outcome, pid, host, heartbeat, and timestamps
Create feedback_command table with project_id, name, and command

description: Enforce task status enum values in the data layer
steps: Support pending, in_progress, blocked, passed, and failed statuses
Reject unknown task statuses in CLI validation
Map passed status to PRD passes=true on export
Map all other statuses to PRD passes=false on export

description: Resolve the current project from the nearest git root and auto-create project records
steps: Walk upward from the current directory to find the nearest git root
Use the git root path as the project identity
Auto-create a project row when no row exists for that root
Use the repository directory name as the default project name
Show an error when no git root can be found

description: Implement project commands
steps: Implement goralph project info
Implement goralph project init with optional --name and --description
Ensure project init updates the auto-created project row when present
Support human output by default and JSON output with --json

description: Load user and project configuration with project overrides
steps: Load user config from XDG config path such as ~/.config/goralph/config.yaml
Load project config from .ralph/config.yaml
Let project config override user config
Support runner and feedback command configuration
Allow db path override via flag, env, and user config

description: Implement strict PRD validation for JSON array files
steps: Implement goralph prd validate <file>
Require top-level JSON array
Require category and description to be non-empty strings
Require steps to be an array of non-empty strings
Require passes to be a boolean
Reject unknown fields
Reject duplicate descriptions within the imported file

description: Implement PRD import with replace-by-default behavior and append support
steps: Implement goralph prd import <file>
Insert imported tasks and steps into the current project
If tasks already exist and running in a TTY, confirm replacement by default
If tasks already exist and running non-interactively, fail unless --replace or --append is provided
Support --replace to replace current project tasks
Support --append to add imported tasks without deleting existing tasks
Assign stable task IDs from the database, not from JSON

description: Implement PRD export in the requested JSON array format
steps: Implement goralph prd export [file]
Export each task with category, description, steps, and passes
Order steps by task_step.position
Set passes true only when task status is passed
Write to stdout when no file is provided
Write valid pretty JSON when a file is provided

description: Implement task listing and inspection commands
steps: Implement goralph task list with optional --status filter
Implement goralph task show <id>
Display steps, status, progress report, timestamps, and latest progress entries
Support --json for machine-readable output

description: Implement task creation and update commands
steps: Implement goralph task add --category --description --step ...
Implement goralph task update <id> for editable fields
Allow task update to change category, description, steps, status, and progress_report
Validate status values before writing
Preserve step ordering

description: Implement task lifecycle shortcut commands
steps: Implement goralph task start <id> to set in_progress
Implement goralph task pass <id> to set passed
Implement goralph task fail <id> --reason to set failed and record progress
Implement goralph task block <id> --reason to set blocked and record progress
Implement goralph task unblock <id> as an alias for setting status pending
Allow goralph task update <id> --status pending as the non-alias unblock path

description: Implement progress logging commands
steps: Implement goralph progress add --task <id> --summary <text>
Allow progress entries without a task when appropriate
Associate progress with the current project
Associate progress with the active run when one exists
Implement goralph progress list with optional --task filter
Support --json output

description: Determine eligible tasks for agent selection
steps: Treat pending tasks as eligible
Treat failed tasks as eligible for retry
Exclude passed tasks
Exclude blocked tasks until manually unblocked
Exclude in_progress tasks from new selection
Return a no eligible tasks outcome when no eligible tasks remain

description: Define a runner interface with a Pi runner implementation
steps: Create a runner interface that can execute a prompt and return run metadata
Implement a Pi-backed runner as the default implementation
Keep the interface open for future non-Pi runners
Allow runner command and args to be configured
Use Pi as the default runner when no custom runner is configured

description: Run Pi headlessly by default and support interactive mode
steps: Use pi -p with the generated prompt for default headless execution
Capture runner stdout, stderr, and exit status
Keep Pi session persistence enabled so sessions can be reopened later
Add --interactive to launch the Pi TUI instead of headless mode
Ensure headless mode is scriptable for automation

description: Stream runner output while preserving run metadata
steps: Stream runner stdout and stderr to the terminal by default
Add --quiet to suppress live streaming
Do not store full runner transcripts in SQLite
Store session IDs and session files for full transcript lookup
Make run show display metadata, progress, and session file paths

description: Track each agent turn as a run record
steps: Create a run row when goralph run one or run all starts a turn
Store runner name, task ID when known, status, timestamps, exit outcome, pid, host, and heartbeat
Store Pi session_id and session_file when available
Update ended_at and status when the turn finishes
Link progress entries to the active run

description: Implement run session inspection commands
steps: Implement goralph run list
Implement goralph run show <id>
Implement goralph run open <id> using pi --session <session_id or file>
Implement goralph run export <id> [file] using Pi session export support
Show clear errors when a run has no stored session metadata

description: Generate the Ralph loop agent prompt contract
steps: Include project name and root path
Include the forced task when --task is provided
Include eligible non-passed tasks when the agent must choose
Include task steps and latest progress context
Include configured feedback commands
Instruct the agent to call goralph task start before work
Instruct the agent to call goralph progress add during or after work
Instruct the agent to call task pass, fail, or block at the end
Instruct the agent to work on only one feature
Instruct the agent to commit the feature
Instruct the agent to output <promise>COMPLETE</promise> when all work is complete

description: Implement goralph run one with agent task selection by default
steps: Implement goralph run one
Load the current project and eligible tasks
Fail with no eligible tasks outcome when none exist
Generate a prompt that lets the agent choose the highest-priority eligible task
Run exactly one agent turn
Verify run metadata and task updates after the turn
Stop after one turn regardless of remaining tasks

description: Allow goralph run one to target a specific task
steps: Add --task <id> to goralph run one
Validate that the task belongs to the current project
Generate a prompt focused on only the specified task
Allow the specified task even when default agent selection would choose another task
Reject missing or unknown task IDs

description: Implement goralph run all for repeated Ralph loop turns
steps: Implement goralph run all
Repeat agent turns until a stop condition occurs
Stop when all tasks are passed
Stop when no eligible tasks remain
Stop when the runner exits non-zero
Stop when the agent outputs <promise>COMPLETE</promise>
Stop when max turns is reached
Stop on blocked or failed task by default
Support --continue-on-blocked to keep working other tasks

description: Reject unsupported task override combinations for run all
steps: Ensure goralph run all --task <id> is invalid
Print a clear validation error
Return the validation error exit outcome
Document that --task is only supported for run one

description: Detect <promise>COMPLETE</promise> during runs without auto-passing tasks
steps: Scan runner stdout and stderr for the exact string <promise>COMPLETE</promise>
Stop run all when the string appears
Report that the agent declared completion
Do not automatically mark remaining tasks passed
Leave remaining task review to the user

description: Require a clean git worktree before running agents
steps: Verify the current project is inside a git repository
Check git status before goralph run one and run all
Fail on a dirty worktree by default
Allow --allow-dirty to bypass the pre-run dirty check
Return the dirty git exit outcome when blocked

description: Verify the agent committed its work after each successful turn
steps: After a runner turn, check the git worktree
Treat a dirty worktree after a successful task update as run failure
Record the failure in the run record
Report that the agent did not leave a clean committed state
Do not create commits from the CLI

description: Prevent concurrent runs within the same project
steps: Allow only one active run per project
Fail run one and run all when another active run exists
Store pid, host, and heartbeat_at for active runs
Return the active run exists exit outcome
Expose enough information for the user to inspect the active run

description: Handle stale active runs with PID and heartbeat detection
steps: Update heartbeat_at while a runner subprocess is alive
Detect dead pids on the same host
Detect heartbeat entries older than --stale-after
Require --force to mark stale runs failed before starting a new run
Require --force for cross-host stale run recovery

description: Protect against unsafe command execution from PRD files
steps: Do not execute commands from imported PRD content
Only execute runner and feedback commands from trusted config
Show configured commands before running them when appropriate
Require --yes or --force for destructive non-TTY actions
Avoid logging secrets beyond what the runner itself prints

description: Implement feedback command configuration and execution
steps: Implement goralph feedback list
Implement goralph feedback set <name> <command>
Implement goralph feedback run [name]
Store feedback commands per project
Let the agent decide which feedback commands to run from prompt context
Support manual feedback execution from the CLI

description: Do not enforce feedback command success in v1 automated runs
steps: Include feedback commands in the generated agent prompt
Instruct the agent to run relevant feedback loops before passing a task
Do not automatically run feedback commands after the agent exits in v1
Leave command success reporting to progress entries and task status updates

description: Support human output and JSON output across commands
steps: Use human-readable tables or summaries by default
Add a global --json flag
Emit stable machine-readable JSON for command results when --json is set
Write errors to stderr
Avoid human text on stdout in JSON mode

description: Emit NDJSON events for run commands in JSON mode
steps: When --json is used with run commands, output newline-delimited JSON events
Emit run_started events
Emit task_selected events when a task is selected or forced
Emit progress_added events when observable
Emit runner_output events for streamed runner output
Emit run_completed or run_failed events at the end
Do not mix human text into stdout in NDJSON mode

description: Represent command outcomes with named exit enums
steps: Define named outcomes for success, generic error, validation error, no eligible tasks, dirty git, active run exists, runner failed, feedback failed, and task blocked or failed stop
Map named outcomes to process exit codes internally
Use outcome names in JSON output
Use consistent outcomes across all commands

description: Add PRD validation and import tests
steps: Test valid PRD JSON validation succeeds
Test unknown fields are rejected
Test missing required fields are rejected
Test duplicate descriptions are rejected
Test import replace and append behavior against a temp database

description: Add database integration tests with temporary SQLite databases
steps: Run Goose migrations against temp SQLite files
Verify schema constraints and foreign keys
Verify sqlc query methods for projects, tasks, steps, progress, runs, and feedback commands
Keep tests isolated from the user's real global database

description: Add CLI command tests with temporary directories and environment
steps: Run commands with a temp GO_RALPH_DB
Use temp git repositories for project resolution tests
Test --json output shape for representative commands
Test non-TTY destructive command protections
Verify command tests do not depend on real user config

description: Add fake runner tests for run one and run all
steps: Implement a fake runner for tests
Test run one executes exactly one turn
Test run one --task forces the specified task
Test run all stops on all passed
Test run all stops on <promise>COMPLETE</promise>
Test run all stops on blocked or failed by default
Ensure tests never call real Pi

description: Add git safety tests
steps: Create temp git repositories in tests
Verify dirty pre-run worktrees are rejected
Verify --allow-dirty bypasses pre-run rejection
Verify dirty post-run worktrees mark runs failed
Verify clean committed post-run state is accepted

description: Add concurrency and stale run tests
steps: Test active run detection prevents a second run in the same project
Test stale run detection using dead same-host pid
Test stale run detection using old heartbeat
Test --force marks stale runs failed before starting a new run
Test cross-host stale recovery requires force

description: Write README quickstart and core workflow documentation
steps: Document installation or build steps
Document project auto-detection from git root
Document PRD import and export workflow
Document run one and run all behavior
Document the agent progress and task update contract

description: Write command reference documentation
steps: Document project commands
Document prd commands
Document task commands including unblock
Document progress commands
Document run commands and session inspection commands
Document feedback commands
Document db commands and global flags

description: Document configuration examples
steps: Provide an example ~/.config/goralph/config.yaml
Provide an example .ralph/config.yaml
Show default Pi runner configuration
Show feedback command configuration
Document config precedence and database path overrides

description: Document the PRD JSON schema and example
steps: Show the required JSON array structure
Describe category, description, steps, and passes fields
Explain strict validation rules
Explain how passes maps to internal task status
Include a small valid PRD example

description: Document developer setup and quality commands
steps: Document how to install sqlc and Goose tooling
Document how to regenerate sqlc code
Document how to run migrations in development
Document go test ./... as the baseline verification command
Document that tests must not invoke real Pi
